action: C1 ship-gate tree-SHA binding: audit emits audit_bound_tree_sha (git -C $WORKTREE rev-parse HEAD^{tree} before writing audit-report.md); scripts/lifecycle/ship.sh captures tree_sha_committed post-commit and asserts equality with AUDIT_BOUND_TREE_SHA env; scripts/guards/ship-gate.sh refuses if env unset or mismatched (loud BREACH failure); scripts/lifecycle/phase-gate.sh gate_audit_to_ship extracts audit_bound_tree_sha via regex from audit-report.md prose and exports as env for ship.sh; subagent-run.sh ledger writer adds both SHA fields to ship entries; NEW scripts/observability/detect-tree-sha-breach.sh scans ledger for past mismatches. ~150 LoC. Files: agents/evolve-auditor.md, scripts/lifecycle/ship.sh (lines 479-487 + 614-680), scripts/guards/ship-gate.sh, scripts/lifecycle/phase-gate.sh (lines 625-732), scripts/dispatch/subagent-run.sh, NEW scripts/observability/detect-tree-sha-breach.sh.
operator_note: Closes c38 incident breach class. Verification: (1) detect-tree-sha-breach.sh must surface commit 55d1dc5 as historical breach (positive control — its message claims c38 script changes but actual tree contains only inbox/*.json additions). (2) Synthetic test: modify file in test worktree post-audit, ship-gate refuses. (3) Happy-path regression: one PASS cycle, both SHAs match, ledger contains both. Standalone fix — does NOT require schema framework (C2). Uses regex extraction of audit_bound_tree_sha from audit-report.md until C3 promotes to JSON. Anti-goals: no schema framework changes, no profile changes beyond auditor, no kernel hook restructuring beyond ship-gate addition. Critical-path fix for live integrity hole.
